popularity_bias.concern: Zero bridging papers found. Cuproptosis-bacteria literature is only about copper nanomaterial antibacterials, not chemolithotroph copper biology.

vagueness.concern: FDX1-ferredoxin is real homology. DLAT homolog present. BUT: DLST absent (incomplete TCA in A. ferrooxidans). CRY-Fe-S cuproptosis function untested for bacterial ferredoxins.

structural_impossibility.concern: No fundamental barrier. pH mismatch moderate (intracellular 6.5 vs 7.2). Periplasmic mechanisms not transferable; cytoplasmic mechanisms ARE. Macomber & Imlay 2009 confirms convergent copper-Fe-S disruption.

local_optima.concern: Fully distinct from all prior sessions. No copper/chemolithotroph overlap. Bridge types (enzymatic cascade, tool transfer) historically survive.

popularity_bias.concern: DISJOINTNESS FAILED. 4-6 explicit bridge papers found: Zhang 2026 (Science Advances), Bollen 2025 (Nature Communications), Pei 2025 (Nature Microbiology), Ortiz-Rodriguez 2024. Active convergence in 2024-2026.

vagueness.concern: Specific bridge concepts verified (TisB aggregation, DnaK/ClpB, antitoxin IDRs). Sub-hypotheses remain novel (ppGpp-condensate, TA-IDR scaffolding, lysine acetylation triple bridge).

structural_impossibility.concern: No barriers. Persister environment favors LLPS (low ATP, high crowding). Antitoxins have IDRs. Biophysically sound.

local_optima.concern: Condensate field reused from Session 1 (Bioelectric x Condensates). Framework re-description risk — close to vocabulary re-description, a historically failing bridge type.

deal_breakers: Disjointness verification failed: 4-6 bridging papers published 2024-2026

popularity_bias.concern: 14-year gap since Mandilaras & Missirlis 2012 Drosophila screen. Zero mammalian Fe-S biogenesis x circadian papers. Nadimpalli 2024 (IRP1/2 diurnal) is closest but focuses on iron homeostasis broadly.

vagueness.concern: CISD2 (PDB:3FNV), NFS1, GLRX5 all named with structures. Two NFS1 inhibitors in 2025 (Zhu: Compound 53, Zangari: D-cysteine). CRY-Fe-S bridge is weak (CRY uses FAD, not Fe-S) — de-emphasize.

structural_impossibility.concern: Nernst math verified: 30mV/n=1 gives 3.07x shift at 37C. Labile cluster half-lives in hours range (NEET proteins). NAD+/NADH 30% amplitude over 24h confirmed (Peek 2013). No energy scale or compartment mismatch.

local_optima.concern: Zero overlap with any prior session. Uses network_gap_analysis (best strategy, 43% QG pass rate). Bridge types: indirect enzymatic cascade + quantitative thermodynamic framework — both historically survive.

recommendation_rationale: Target 3 (Fe-S cluster biogenesis x Circadian clock regulation) scores 9/10 — highest across all axes. 14-year novelty gap with zero mammalian follow-up on Drosophila lead. Nernst quantitative framework verified. Two NFS1 tool compounds published 2025. Uses pipeline's best strategy (network_gap_analysis). Target 2 downgraded from 8 to 5: disjointness claim FALSE (4-6 bridging papers 2024-2026). Target 1 solid backup at 7/10 but narrower scope (DLST absent). Generator should de-emphasize CRY-Fe-S (CRY uses FAD) and strengthen CISD2/PRX/NFS1 bridges.
